M-081.NAME: vpn-device-abuse-alerts
M-081.owns: Durable VPN device abuse alert inbox for confirmed anti-sharing signals, operator review, safe device-only enforcement actions, and archived resolutions.
M-081.purpose: Turn VPN anti-abuse audit events into explicit admin-reviewable alerts without enabling automatic punishment by default.

exports.model-VPNDeviceAbuseAlert: Persistent alert row linked to user, device, source security event, signal type, severity, status, occurrence count, and reviewed action outcome.
exports.fn-create-device-abuse-alert: Create or update a deduplicated open alert from ping-pong or multi-network VPN abuse evidence.
exports.fn-list-device-abuse-alerts: Return paginated open/resolved alerts for the admin cockpit.
exports.fn-get-device-abuse-alert: Return one alert with safe user/device/context detail for operator review.
exports.fn-resolve-device-abuse-alert: Close an alert without changing runtime access after admin review.
exports.fn-rotate-device-for-alert: Rotate only the affected device config after explicit admin confirmation.
exports.fn-block-device-for-alert: Block only the affected device peer after explicit admin confirmation.

alert-policy.policy-1: Only confirmed ping_pong_abuse_detected and multi_network_abuse_detected events create operator alerts.
alert-policy.policy-2: Normal roaming, port-only NAT churn, Redis degraded events, and generic warnings must not create alert-inbox noise.
alert-policy.policy-3: Alerts never auto-rotate or block access by themselves; every action requires explicit admin confirmation.
alert-policy.policy-4: Enforcement actions target exactly one device_id and must not disable the user account or unrelated devices.
alert-policy.policy-5: Resolved alerts leave the open inbox and remain queryable through an archive list.

data-contract.field-1: Alert payload may include user id, user email, device id, device name, config version, last endpoint, last handshake, signal type, severity, timestamps, and safe occurrence counters.
data-contract.field-2: Alert payloads and audit details must never include VPN private keys, preshared keys, full config bodies, bearer tokens, or raw high-risk request payloads.
data-contract.field-3: Raw endpoint IPs may be shown only as the existing admin device endpoint context; no broader IP history is introduced by this module.

constraints.constraint-1: ANTI_ABUSE_MODE remains observe by default; Phase-78 is an admin visibility and reviewed-action phase, not an auto-punishment rollout.
constraints.constraint-2: Schema migration must be additive and backward-compatible with SQLModel.create_all transition behavior.
constraints.constraint-3: Admin UI must stay compact on mobile and preserve the Phase-75 unified Users inventory.

M-081.hotspot: H-068: VPN abuse alerts must be high-signal and device-scoped so normal mobile roaming is not treated as fraud and admins cannot accidentally punish a whole account.
M-081.evidence: PASS local: M-081 model/service/API/UI/smoke implemented; confirmed events create deduped alerts; resolve/rotate/block are confirmation-safe and device-scoped; protected deploy/runtime/user-frontend guard passed.
